samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_8bit">
    <description>Implement an 8-bit multiplier based on shifting and adding operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="A" width_description="[7:0]">First 8-bit input operand (representing a multiplicand).</input>
        <input id="B" width_description="[7:0]">Second 8-bit input operand (representing a multiplier).</input>
        <output id="product" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit output representing the product of the two 8-bit inputs (A * B).</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <lines>
            <line id="counter">Counter for shifting and adding operations.</line>
            <line id="add">Addition operation.</line>
            <line id="shift">Shifting operation.</line>
        </lines>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <assign>
            <line id="counter">counter = 0;</line>
            <line id="add">add = 0;</line>
            <line id="shift">shift = 0;</line>
        </assign>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module performs multiplication of A and B using the shift-and-add method.
    - The algorithm iterates through each bit of the multiplier (B). For each bit that is set (1), the multiplicand (A) is added to the product at the corresponding shifted position.
    - The process continues until all bits of the multiplier have been processed.
    - After each addition, the multiplicand is logically shifted left by one bit to prepare for the next addition, simulating the traditional multiplication process.
    </implementation>
</module>
